evals.name: saas-launch-review
evals.prompt: I'm the solo dev on `mailsync` (a multi-tenant SaaS that syncs Gmail and drafts replies with OpenAI; Postgres backend). I want to launch next week. Review the repo and tell me if it's production-ready and what I'm missing. Be specific about the riskiest gaps.
evals.expected_output: Severity-ranked review that catches: no CI, non-atomic delete-then-insert, no 429 backoff, uncapped OpenAI/embedding spend, untested sync orchestrator, secret logged, silent catch. Cites file evidence.

evals.name: new-saas-foundation
evals.prompt: I'm about to start a new B2B SaaS — Next.js + Postgres + Stripe, plus an OpenAI summarization feature. Before I write features, what should I set up from day one so I don't regret it? Give me the foundational checklist.
evals.expected_output: Day-1 foundation: CI from commit 1, typecheck/lint/test, .env.example, data-integrity/RLS decision, config-at-boot, AND cost caps for the OpenAI feature, confirm gate. Prioritized.

evals.name: cli-scope-discipline
evals.prompt: Review my CLI tool `csvfmt` (a tiny Node CLI that pretty-prints a CSV passed as an argument) for production readiness before I publish to npm. What should I fix?
evals.expected_output: Flags missing CI + tests on arg/failure paths + the unguarded missing-file read + publish/release concerns. Respects scope: does NOT flag RLS, dark mode, admin panels, or AI cost caps as gaps.
